(born December 8, 1950) is an American actor and retired [special make-up effects](/wiki/Prosthetic_makeup "Prosthetic makeup") creator.[[1]](#cite_note-filmref-1) He is mostly known for his creature designs and effects. Baker has won the [Academy Award for Best Makeup](/wiki/Academy_Award_for_Best_Makeup_and_Hairstyling "Academy Award for Best Makeup and Hairstyling") a record seven times from a record eleven nominations, beginning with his win in the first year of the category's existence, for the 1981 [horror comedy](/wiki/Comedy_horror "Comedy horror") *[An American Werewolf in London](/wiki/An_American_Werewolf_in_London "An American Werewolf in London")*.[[2]](#cite_note-oscars-2)

**Richard Alan "Rick" Baker** (born December 8, 1950) is an American actor and retired [special make-up effects](/wiki/Prosthetic_makeup "Prosthetic makeup") creator.[[1]](#cite_note-filmref-1) He is mostly known for his creature designs and effects. Baker has won the [Academy Award for Best Makeup](/wiki/Academy_Award_for_Best_Makeup_and_Hairstyling "Academy Award for Best Makeup and Hairstyling") a record seven times from a record eleven nominations, beginning with his win in the first year of the category's existence, for the 1981 [horror comedy](/wiki/Comedy_horror "Comedy horror") *[An American Werewolf in London](/wiki/An_American_Werewolf_in_London "An American Werewolf in London")*.[[2]](#cite_note-oscars-2)

## Early life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Rick_Baker&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early life")]

Baker was born on December 8, 1950, in [Binghamton, New York](/wiki/Binghamton,_New_York "Binghamton, New York"), to Doris ([née](/wiki/Maiden_and_married_names "Maiden and married names") Hamlin), a bank teller, and Ralph B. Baker, a professional artist. He and his family moved to [Covina, California](/wiki/Covina,_California "Covina, California") when he was less than one year old.[[1]](#cite_note-filmref-1)[[3]](#cite_note-fatman-3)

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Rick_Baker&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Career")]

As a teenager, Baker began creating artificial body parts in his own kitchen.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] He also appeared briefly in the fan production *The Night Turkey*, a one-hour, black-and-white video parody of *[The Night Stalker](/wiki/The_Night_Stalker_(1972_film) "The Night Stalker (1972 film)")* (1972), directed by [William Malone](/wiki/William_Malone_(director) "William Malone (director)").[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] Among Baker's first professional jobs was assisting [prosthetic makeup](/wiki/Prosthetic_makeup "Prosthetic makeup") effects veteran [Dick Smith](/wiki/Dick_Smith_(make-up_artist) "Dick Smith (make-up artist)") on the 1973 film *[The Exorcist](/wiki/The_Exorcist "The Exorcist")*.[[2]](#cite_note-oscars-2)[[4]](#cite_note-4) While working on *The Exorcist*, Baker was hired by director [Larry Cohen](/wiki/Larry_Cohen "Larry Cohen") to design and create a [mutant](/wiki/Mutant "Mutant") infant for Cohen's 1974 film *[It's Alive](/wiki/It%27s_Alive_(1974_film) "It's Alive (1974 film)")*.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

At the [54th Academy Awards](/wiki/54th_Academy_Awards "54th Academy Awards"), Baker received the inaugural [Academy Award for Best Makeup](/wiki/Academy_Award_for_Best_Makeup_and_Hairstyling "Academy Award for Best Makeup and Hairstyling") for his work on *[An American Werewolf in London](/wiki/An_American_Werewolf_in_London "An American Werewolf in London")* (1981).[[6]](#cite_note-6) Subsequently, he has been nominated for Best Makeup ten more times, winning on seven occasions, both records in his field.[[2]](#cite_note-oscars-2) Baker also created the [werecat](/wiki/Werecat "Werecat") creature [Michael Jackson](/wiki/Michael_Jackson "Michael Jackson") transforms into in the music video *[Thriller](/wiki/Michael_Jackson%27s_Thriller_(music_video) "Michael Jackson's Thriller (music video)")* (1983).[[7]](#cite_note-7) He would then go on to win his second Academy Award for Best Makeup for the year [1987](/wiki/60th_Academy_Awards "60th Academy Awards"). For Academy Awards for the years [1994](/wiki/67th_Academy_Awards "67th Academy Awards"), [1996](/wiki/69th_Academy_Awards "69th Academy Awards") and [1997](/wiki/70th_Academy_Awards "70th Academy Awards") Baker again win Academy Awards for Best Makeup respectively for his work with *[Ed Wood](/wiki/Ed_Wood "Ed Wood")*, *[The Nutty Professor](/wiki/The_Nutty_Professor_(1996_film) "The Nutty Professor (1996 film)")* and *[Men In Black](/wiki/Men_In_Black "Men In Black")*.[[8]](#cite_note-oscarwinsandnominations-8) For years [2000](/wiki/73rd_Academy_Awards "73rd Academy Awards") and [2010](/wiki/83rd_Academy_Awards "83rd Academy Awards"), he would again receive Academy Awards for Best Makeup for the films *[How The Grinch Stole Christmas](/wiki/How_the_Grinch_Stole_Christmas_(2000_film) "How the Grinch Stole Christmas (2000 film)")* and *[The Wolfman](/wiki/The_Wolfman_(film) "The Wolfman (film)")*.[[8]](#cite_note-oscarwinsandnominations-8)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/29/Rick_Baker_at_Saturn_Awards_%28cropped%29.jpg/250px-Rick_Baker_at_Saturn_Awards_%28cropped%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:Rick_Baker_at_Saturn_Awards_(cropped).jpg)

Baker at the [37th Saturn Awards](/wiki/37th_Saturn_Awards "37th Saturn Awards") in 2011

In 2008, he was awarded a [Doctorate of Humane Letters](/wiki/Doctor_of_Humane_Letters "Doctor of Humane Letters") from the [Academy of Art University](/wiki/Academy_of_Art_University "Academy of Art University") in [San Francisco](/wiki/San_Francisco "San Francisco").[[9]](#cite_note-9) Baker also contributes commentaries to the [web series](/wiki/Web_series "Web series") *[Trailers from Hell](/wiki/Trailers_from_Hell "Trailers from Hell")* for [trailers](/wiki/Trailer_(promotion) "Trailer (promotion)") about horror and science fiction films.[[10]](#cite_note-10) Baker claims that his work on *[Harry and the Hendersons](/wiki/Harry_and_the_Hendersons "Harry and the Hendersons")* (1987) is one of his proudest achievements, for which he won his second Oscar.[[11]](#cite_note-11) On October 3, 2009, he received the [Jack Pierce](/wiki/Jack_Pierce_(make-up_artist) "Jack Pierce (make-up artist)") Lifetime Achievement Award at the Chiller-Eyegore Awards.[[12]](#cite_note-12)

On November 30, 2012, Baker received the 2485th star of the [Hollywood Walk of Fame](/wiki/Hollywood_Walk_of_Fame "Hollywood Walk of Fame"). The star is located in front of the Guinness World Records Museum.[[13]](#cite_note-2485th-13)

On May 28, 2015, Baker announced his retirement, saying: "First of all, the [CG](/wiki/Computer-generated_imagery "Computer-generated imagery") stuff definitely took away the animatronics part of what I do. It's also starting to take away the makeup part. The time is right, I am 64 years old, and the business is crazy right now. I like to do things right, and they wanted cheap and fast. That is not what I want to do, so I just decided it is basically time to get out. I would consider designing and consulting on something, but I don't think I will have a huge working studio anymore."[[14]](#cite_note-14)[[15]](#cite_note-15)[[16]](#cite_note-16)

In 2018, when Baker's daughter Veronica was working at [DC Comics](/wiki/DC_Comics "DC Comics"), he was approached by DC to design a collectible display bust. He agreed on the condition of total creative freedom; working with his long-time mold maker Rob Freitas, he created a bust of [The Joker](/wiki/Joker_(character) "Joker (character)").[[17]](#cite_note-17)

## Acting roles

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Rick_Baker&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Acting roles")]

Baker played the [title role](/wiki/King_Kong "King Kong") in the [1976 remake of *King Kong*](/wiki/King_Kong_(1976_film) "King Kong (1976 film)"). He had initially only been hired to create an ape suit that would fill in for scenes where it was not practical to use a life-sized mechanical version of Kong that had been designed by [Carlo Rambaldi](/wiki/Carlo_Rambaldi "Carlo Rambaldi"), but problems with Rambaldi's creation resulted in Baker playing Kong on-screen for almost all of the film.[[18]](#cite_note-18) In the [2005 remake](/wiki/King_Kong_(2005_film) "King Kong (2005 film)"), he had a cameo as the pilot and gunner (with director [Peter Jackson](/wiki/Peter_Jackson "Peter Jackson")) who shot down Kong. He has also made cameo appearances in: [Michael Jackson](/wiki/Michael_Jackson "Michael Jackson")'s music video *[Thriller](/wiki/Michael_Jackson%27s_Thriller_(music_video) "Michael Jackson's Thriller (music video)")* (1983) as "Zombie Opening the Crypt"; *[Into the Night](/wiki/Into_the_Night_(1985_film) "Into the Night (1985 film)")* (1985) as a drug dealer with a business card; *[Men in Black II](/wiki/Men_in_Black_II "Men in Black II")* (2002) as "MIB Passport Control Agent", an MIB agent helping provide aliens with disguises; *[Men in Black 3](/wiki/Men_in_Black_3 "Men in Black 3")* (2012) as "Brain Alien"; *[The Wolfman](/wiki/The_Wolfman_(2010_film) "The Wolfman (2010 film)")* (2010) as "Gypsy Man / First Killed"; [*The Strain*](/wiki/The_Strain_(TV_series) "The Strain (TV series)") as a convenience store customer (2014, Episode 8, uncredited) and *[Rings](/wiki/Rings_(2017_film) "Rings (2017 film)")* (2017) as a flea market vendor.[[19]](#cite_note-19)

## Personal life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Rick_Baker&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Personal life")]

Baker is married; he was previously married to Elaine Melba Parkyn for ten years. He met his second wife, hairstylist Silvia Abascal, while they were both working on *[Into the Night](/wiki/Into_the_Night_(1985_film) "Into the Night (1985 film)")* (1985).[[3]](#cite_note-fatman-3) Baker and Abascal had their first daughter 1989 and their second in 1993.[[20]](#cite_note-20)

## Selected filmography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Rick_Baker&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Selected filmography")]

* *[Octaman](/wiki/Octaman "Octaman")* (1971) (Octaman costume; co-created with Doug Beswick)[[21]](#cite_note-21)
* *[The Thing with Two Heads](/wiki/The_Thing_with_Two_Heads "The Thing with Two Heads")* (1972) (special effects - uncredited)
* *[Schlock](/wiki/Schlock_(film) "Schlock (film)")* (1973) (makeup artist / actor)
* *[Live and Let Die](/wiki/Live_and_Let_Die_(film) "Live and Let Die (film)")* (1973) (makeup artist - inflated Kananga, dummy Samedi, Mr Big - uncredited)
* *[The Exorcist](/wiki/The_Exorcist "The Exorcist")* (1973) (special effects assistant - uncredited)
* *[It's Alive](/wiki/It%27s_Alive_(1974_film) "It's Alive (1974 film)")* (1974) (makeup artist / special makeup effects artist - uncredited)
* *[The Autobiography of Miss Jane Pittman](/wiki/The_Autobiography_of_Miss_Jane_Pittman_(film) "The Autobiography of Miss Jane Pittman (film)")* (1974) (makeup creator: [Miss Tyson](/wiki/Cicely_Tyson "Cicely Tyson"))
* *[King Kong](/wiki/King_Kong_(1976_film) "King Kong (1976 film)")* (1976) (makeup effects - uncredited / special contributions: [Kong](/wiki/King_Kong "King Kong") / actor)
* *[Track of the Moon Beast](/wiki/Track_of_the_Moon_Beast "Track of the Moon Beast")* (1976) (makeup artist)
* *[Squirm](/wiki/Squirm "Squirm")* (1976) (makeup designer)
* *[The Incredible Melting Man](/wiki/The_Incredible_Melting_Man "The Incredible Melting Man")* (1977) (special makeup effects / actor)
* *[Star Wars](/wiki/Star_Wars_(film) "Star Wars (film)")* (1977) (makeup: second unit)
